name: Tom

profile.shopping_behaviour: Marcus researches exhaustively, comparing Iron Heart, Filson, and The Real McCoy's. Shops at Rivet and Hide or brand websites; visits London stockists like Clutch Café. Never returns items.
profile.personality: Marcus demands quality backed by action, not marketing. Loves patina, triple-stitched seams, copper rivets, and garment stories. Despises premium brands cutting corners—views Belstaff's eyelet rust as heritage betrayal.
profile.style_identity: Function-first workwear aesthetic: selvedge denim, waxed canvas, long-lasting boots. Favors Buzz Rickson's, Warehouse & Co, Filson, Red Wing, Barbour. Owns 3-4 jeans chosen for weight and fade; views each piece as decades-long investment.

preview_description: Marcus, 37, is a Bristol-based heritage workwear devotee who invests in quality basics: Iron Heart raw denim, Barbour jackets, five-year-old Red Wing boots, and £400 Filson bags designed for durability. He researches Japanese mills obsessively, documents denim fades online, and believes premium pieces should last decades without planned obsolescence.

preview_attributes.key_brands: Iron Heart, Barbour, Filson, Red Wing, The Real McCoy's, Warehouse & Co
preview_attributes.style_identity: Heritage workwear purist focused on durability and patina
preview_attributes.shopping_triggers: Construction quality, fabric weight, authenticity, and proven longevity over decades
preview_attributes.pain_points: Brands that charge premium prices but compromise on quality or customer service, stretch denim, fast fashion mentality
